content.content:
1	/**
2	 * Runtime data-access API interfaces — the "two typed surfaces generated from
3	 * one schema".
4	 *
5	 * In the **agent** sandbox `db.*` is a **synchronous** host call (execShell-class;
6	 * fast local SQLite in the same process) — {@link DbApi}. On the **Node** side
7	 * (api/hook handlers) the same `db` is a **cross-thread proxy**, so every method
8	 * is `async` — {@link AsyncDbApi}, derived from `DbApi` so the two can never drift.
9	 * `libs/core` owns only these interfaces; `libs/cli` provides the `better-sqlite3`
10	 * implementations.
11	 */
12	
13	import type { ColumnSchema, TableSchema } from './schema.js';
14	
15	/** A database row — a bag of column values keyed by column name. */
16	export type Row = Record<string, unknown>;
17	
18	/** Options for {@link DbApi.query}. */
19	export interface QueryOpts {
20	  /** Equality filter: each key/value must match the row's column value. */
21	  where?: Record<string, unknown>;
22	  /** Named relations to expand inline (a join under the hood, e.g. `['comments']`). */
23	  include?: string[];
24	  /**
25	   * Sort order. Three accepted shapes:
26	   * - `'issued_date'` — a column name (ascending).
27	   * - `{ column: 'issued_date', dir: 'desc' }` — explicit column + direction.
28	   * - `{ issued_date: 'desc' }` — the column→direction MAP. Every agent writes this shape (it is
29	   *   what the appbuilder's own instruct teaches), so the runtime must honour it: before, it fell
30	   *   through to `opts.orderBy.column === undefined` and every authored list route answered 500
31	   *   while the raw `app/data/<table>` API — which passes no `orderBy` — looked perfectly healthy.
32	   */
33	  orderBy?: string | { column: string; dir?: 'asc' | 'desc' } | Record<string, 'asc' | 'desc'>;
34	  /** Maximum number of rows to return. */
35	  limit?: number;
36	  /** Number of leading rows to skip (for pagination). */
37	  offset?: number;
38	}
39	
40	/** Options for {@link DbApi.update}. */
41	export interface UpdateOpts {
42	  /** Equality filter selecting the rows to update. */
43	  where: Record<string, unknown>;
44	  /** Column/value pairs to assign to the matched rows. */
45	  set: Record<string, unknown>;
46	}
47	
48	/** Options for {@link DbApi.remove}. */
49	export interface RemoveOpts {
50	  /** Equality filter selecting the rows to delete. */
51	  where: Record<string, unknown>;
52	}
53	
54	/**
55	 * The **synchronous** agent-side data API (execShell-class host call). Available
56	 * in the agent sandbox where SQLite runs in the same process, so no turn
57	 * boundary is crossed. The Node-side mirror is {@link AsyncDbApi}.
58	 *
59	 * Method availability is gated per agent by capabilities: `db:read`
60	 * (`query`/`tables`), `db:write` (`insert`/`update`/`remove`), and `db:schema`
61	 * (`createTable`/`addColumn`).
62	 */
63	export interface DbApi {
64	  /**
65	   * Read rows from a table, optionally filtered, sorted, paginated, and with
66	   * named relations expanded inline.
67	   * @param table The table name (file basename).
68	   * @param opts  Query filter/sort/pagination/include options.
69	   * @returns The matching rows.
70	   */
71	  query(table: string, opts?: QueryOpts): Row[];
72	
73	  /**
74	   * List the names of all tables in the project's database.
75	   * @returns The table names.
76	   */
77	  tables(): string[];
78	
79	  /**
80	   * Insert one row or a batch of rows, filling in `generated`/`default` values.
81	   * @param table  The table name.
82	   * @param values A single row or an array of rows.
83	   * @returns The inserted row(s), shape mirroring the input (single in → single out).
84	   */
85	  insert(table: string, values: Row | Row[]): Row | Row[];
86	
87	  /**
88	   * Update the columns of every row matching the filter.
89	   * @param table The table name.
90	   * @param opts  The `where` filter and `set` assignments.
91	   * @returns The number of rows updated.
92	   */
93	  update(table: string, opts: UpdateOpts): number;
94	
95	  /**
96	   * Delete every row matching the filter.
97	   * @param table The table name.
98	   * @param opts  The `where` filter.
99	   * @returns The number of rows deleted.
100	   */
101	  remove(table: string, opts: RemoveOpts): number;
102	
103	  /**
104	   * Create a new table from a schema (`CREATE TABLE` + relations). Authoring
105	   * capability (`db:schema`) — evolves the data model live.
106	   * @param schema The full table schema (the table name comes from `schema.title`/basename convention at the impl layer).
107	   */
108	  createTable(schema: TableSchema): void;
109	
110	  /**
111	   * Add a column to an existing table (`ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN`; additive-lenient
112	   * evolution). Authoring capability (`db:schema`).
113	   * @param table  The table to alter.
114	   * @param name   The new column's name.
115	   * @param column The new column's schema.
116	   */
117	  addColumn(table: string, name: string, column: ColumnSchema): void;
118	}
119	
120	/**
121	 * The **asynchronous** Node-side data API for api/hook handlers — the same
122	 * method set as {@link DbApi} but every method returns a `Promise` of the same
123	 * result (a cross-thread proxy, so callers `await`). Derived from `DbApi` by a
124	 * mapped type so the two surfaces can never drift.
125	 */
126	export type AsyncDbApi = {
127	  [K in keyof DbApi]: (...args: Parameters<DbApi[K]>) => Promise<ReturnType<DbApi[K]>>;
128	};
129	
130	/**
131	 * Call a named project/external endpoint by name (the agent/Node named-endpoint
132	 * call). External services are named bindings (hidden URL + key); own-project
133	 * endpoints resolve in-process. Gated by the `api:call` allowlist. Typed
134	 * overloads per endpoint are generated later (Phase 4).
135	 * @param name  The endpoint name from the `api:call` allowlist.
136	 * @param input The endpoint input payload.
137	 * @returns The endpoint's result.
138	 */
139	export type ApiCallFn = (name: string, input?: unknown) => Promise<unknown>;
140	
141	/**
142	 * A single authenticated request to a connected external service, made through
143	 * the gateway egress proxy. `path` is ALWAYS relative to the provider's API base
144	 * (the gateway pins the host and rejects absolute URLs); the OAuth token is
145	 * attached server-side and never enters the pod or the sandbox.
146	 */
147	export interface ConnectionRequest {
148	  /** HTTP method (GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE). */
149	  method: string;
150	  /** Path relative to the provider's API base, e.g. `/gmail/v1/users/me/messages`. */
151	  path: string;
152	  /** Query-string params appended to the URL. */
153	  query?: Record<string, string>;
154	  /** JSON request body (serialized by the proxy). */
155	  body?: unknown;
156	  /** Extra request headers (an `Authorization` header here is ignored — the
157	   *  gateway attaches the connection's token). */
158	  headers?: Record<string, string>;
159	}
160	
161	/** The proxy's normalized response — `data` is parsed JSON (or text on non-JSON). */
162	export interface ConnectionResponse {
163	  ok: boolean;
164	  status: number;
165	  data: unknown;
166	}
167	
168	/**
169	 * Resolve a `callConnection()` yield — forward a {@link ConnectionRequest} to the
170	 * gateway's egress proxy for the named provider and return its normalized
171	 * response. Host-supplied (libs/cli, from the pod's scoped connections JWT);
172	 * absent outside a pod with a configured connections gateway, in which case a
173	 * `callConnection` yield rejects with a clear, retryable error.
174	 * @param provider The connection provider id (e.g. `google`/`slack`/`github`).
175	 * @param req      The request to forward.
176	 * @returns The proxy response.
177	 */
178	export type ConnectionResolver = (
179	  provider: string,
180	  req: ConnectionRequest,
181	) => Promise<ConnectionResponse>;
182	
183	/**
184	 * Fire-and-forget spawn of an agent action from a Node handler. Returns
185	 * immediately with a run id; failures surface via the optional `onError`.
186	 * @param ref   The action reference, shaped `'space/agent#action'`.
187	 * @param input The action input payload.
188	 * @param opts  Optional handlers — `onError` is invoked if the run fails.
189	 * @returns A handle carrying the spawned `runId`.
190	 */
191	export type SpawnFn = (
192	  ref: string,
193	  input?: unknown,
194	  opts?: { onError?: (err: unknown) => void },
195	) => { runId: string };
196

libs/core/system-spaces/system-appbuilder/knowledge/app_building/model/capability-model.md:13:| `db:schema` | `writeTableSchema`, `db.createTable`/`db.addColumn` | optional `{ tables: [...] }` |

=== docs runtime-globals api-call ===
../../org/docs/runtime-globals/app-authoring.md:1:# App-authoring globals — `writeTableSchema` · `writeApi` · `writePage` · `writeHook` · `createProject` · `selectProject` · `apiCall`
../../org/docs/runtime-globals/app-authoring.md:3:The project-app authoring globals let a capability-holding agent **write the files of a project application** — table schemas, API handlers, React pages, hooks, emitter defs, project functions — and (via `apiCall`) **call the project's own API endpoints**. They are the sandbox side of the [project format](../format/project/README.md).
../../org/docs/runtime-globals/app-authoring.md:7:- **Every writer is a SYNCHRONOUS host call, not a yield.** They are bound straight onto the VM with `injectGlobal` and their DTS declares a plain object return, not a `Promise` `sdk/org/libs/core/src/exec/app-globals.ts:20-27` · `sdk/org/libs/core/src/typecheck/library-dts.ts:189-233`. Only `apiCall` yields — it ends the turn because the host has to run a Node handler `sdk/org/libs/core/src/globals/api-call.ts:1-27`.
../../org/docs/runtime-globals/app-authoring.md:28:| `api:call` | `apiCall(name, input?)` — **yields** | same | — (calls, does not write) | [api/](../format/project/api/README.md) |
../../org/docs/runtime-globals/app-authoring.md:113:**`writeProjectPage` guards a destructive overwrite.** It takes an optional third argument, `opts?: { replace?: boolean }` `sdk/org/libs/cli/src/app/authoring/globals.ts#writeProjectPage`. Without `replace`, a write onto an **existing** page whose source fetches ≥1 API route — every `useApi` / `useApiMutation` / `apiCall` with a literal route, per `fetchedRoutes` — is **rejected** when the incoming source fetches *none* of those routes `sdk/org/libs/cli/src/app/authoring/globals.ts#wouldDropData`. A `@app/runtime` page reaches the database only through those hooks, so a replacement that drops them all renders nothing: the app still builds and every route still answers 200, while the user opens it to an empty page. (Live in scenario 07: a later "add an invoices section" turn re-authored `pages/index.tsx`, and a household's renewals/policies/accounts dashboard came back as `Home · [Invoices]` — with `/vault-dashboard` still serving the whole household to a page that no longer fetched it.) A first write, a page that fetched nothing anyway, and a rewrite that keeps ≥1 of its routes all pass; `{ replace: true }` is the explicit "the user asked me to remove those sections".
../../org/docs/runtime-globals/app-authoring.md:127:## `apiCall(name, input?) → Promise<any>` — `api:call`
../../org/docs/runtime-globals/app-authoring.md:129:The one **value-yielding** app global: it ends the turn, the host runs the endpoint (a worker-isolated Node handler), and the result is bound back into the VM `sdk/org/libs/core/src/globals/api-call.ts:1-27`. It is dual-addressed with the browser path — the page's `useApi` and the agent's `apiCall` enter the *same* main-process API runtime, the agent by endpoint **`name`** `sdk/org/libs/cli/src/app/api/runtime.ts:100-110`.
../../org/docs/runtime-globals/app-authoring.md:141:declare function apiCall(name: 'markRead', input: { id: string }): Promise<{ ok: boolean }>;
../../org/docs/runtime-globals/app-authoring.md:149:apiCall is not available here: this session has no project api runtime
../../org/docs/runtime-globals/data-db.md:174:The practical consequence for an agent: `db.*` never ends the turn, so a read-modify-write sequence runs inside one statement batch — but `apiCall` **does** yield, so anything routed through an endpoint crosses a turn boundary. Reach for `db` for direct row work an agent is granted; reach for `apiCall` when the project has already encoded the operation (transactions, non-equality filters, validation) as an endpoint.

summary:
I have what I need. Here is the precise, per-capability report.

---

# 1. `apiCall(name, input)` — REAL, but only THING holds the grant

**(a) Exists.** **(b)** Global factory: `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/core/src/globals/api-call.ts` (`createApiCallGlobal`). It is a **YIELD**, kind `'apiCall'`:

```ts
pushYield({ kind: 'apiCall', args: [name, input], deferred: { resolve, reject }, vmPromiseHandle: undefined });
```

Routed in `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/core/src/eval/yield-router.ts:190-210`. Kind is in the union at `libs/core/src/eval/yield.ts:4`. Host resolver: `libs/cli/src/app/api/runtime.ts` (`callByName`) — re-enters the *same* main-process api runtime as the browser's `useApi`.

**(c) Capability:** `api:call` — and it is **allowlist-gated, and the allowlist is REQUIRED** (a bare `api:call` fails the space load; `libs/core/src/spaces/capabilities.ts`). Enforcement is in the router, not at injection:

```ts
const allow = ctx.apiCallAllow;
if (allow && !allow.includes('*') && !allow.includes(name)) {
  throw new Error(`apiCall("${name}") is not permitted: this agent's api:call allowlist is [${allow.join(', ')}]`);
}
```

**(d) DTS:** `libs/core/src/typecheck/library-dts.ts:175`
`declare function apiCall(name: string, input?: unknown): Promise<any>;` — replaced by generated per-endpoint overloads when the project supplies `appDts`.

**(e) Harness observation (HTTP only):** `GET /api/sessions/:id/events?format=json` returns **TraceEvents**; you will see `{type:'yield', kind:'apiCall', args:[name, input]}` and a matching `{type:'yield_resolved', kind:'apiCall', value}`. `scenarios/harness/lib/thing.mjs` already exposes `didYield('apiCall')` and `turn().yields`. It is **not** merely a `statement` event.

**Who holds it today:** ONLY `user-thing/thing` — `libs/core/system-spaces/user-thing/agents/thing/instruct.md:9`: `- api:call: { allow: ['*'] }`. **The automator, app-architect, api-author, page-builder, data-modeler hold NO `api:call`.** An Act asserting the *automator* calling `apiCall` is a fantasy; asserting THING doing it is real.

**Docs page:** `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/org/docs/runtime-globals/app-authoring.md:127` (`## apiCall(name, input?) → Promise<any> — api:call`) plus `data-db.md:174`, `README.md:251`.

---

# 2. `db.query` `include` — REAL for agents/handlers; NOT exposed by the admin data API

**(a) Exists.** **(b)** `QueryOpts.include?: string[]` (`libs/core/src/db/types.ts:23`); expansion in `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/app/store.ts:473-506` (`expandIncludes` — `belongsTo` → object, `hasMany` → array).

**Declaration shape** (`database/<table>.json`, `libs/core/src/db/schema.ts:61-107`):

```json
{
  "title": "Orders",
  "description": "One order the user placed.",
  "columns": {
    "id": { "type": "string", "description": "PK", "primaryKey": true, "generated": "uuid" }
  },
  "relations": {
    "items":  { "hasMany": "order_items", "via": "order_id", "description": "the lines on this order" },
    "parent": { "belongsTo": "orders",    "via": "order_id", "description": "the order this line is on" }
  }
}
```
`description` on a relation is **required** (`libs/core/src/db/validate.ts:71-78`).

**(c)** `db:read` (query lives in `DB_READ_MEMBERS`). **(d) DTS** (`library-dts.ts:154`): `query(table, opts?: { where?; include?: string[]; orderBy?; limit?; offset? }): any[]`.

**Writer accepts relations?** YES — `writeProjectTable` calls `validateTableSchema(name, schema)` (`libs/cli/src/app/authoring/globals.ts:605`), which validates `relations` (description only, single-table). The automator is *explicitly taught* the relation shape: `libs/core/system-spaces/system-appbuilder/agents/automator/instruct.md:198-230` ("DECLARE THE RELATION…").
⚠️ **Sharp edge:** cross-table resolution (`relation targets unknown table "X"`) only runs at *set* validation (`validate.ts:154-162`, via `libs/cli/src/app/loader.ts:97`) — a relation to a table that doesn't exist yet passes the writer and fails the whole project db at boot.

**(e) Harness observation:** **The served admin data API does NOT expand relations.** `handleListRows` (`/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/server/routes/app-admin.ts:344-377`) passes only paging:
```ts
const rows = pdb.db.query(table, { limit, offset });
```
So `GET /api/projects/:id/app/data/:table` gives you flat rows. To observe an expansion over HTTP you must go through a **project API handler** the agent authored (`ctx.db.query(t, { include: [...] })`) and hit it via `pod.appApi(projectId, route, body)`. Alternatively assert the *declaration* by `GET /api/fs/read?path=<project>/database/<t>.json` and check `.relations`.

---

# 3. `db.createTable` / `db.addColumn` — REAL API, GRANTED to the automator, but the instructions never teach it → **this is the round's product bug**

**(a) Exists, live DDL.** **(b)** `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/app/store.ts:510-524`:

```ts
function createTable(schema: TableSchema): void {
  const name = slugifyTitle(schema.title);
  raw.exec(schemaToCreateTableSql(name, schema));
  registry.set(name, cloneSchema(schema));
}
function addColumn(table: string, name: string, column: ColumnSchema): void {
  raw.exec(`ALTER TABLE ${ident(table)} ADD COLUMN ${columnDefSql(name, column, true)}`);
  const schema = registry.get(table);
  if (schema) schema.columns[name] = column;
}
```
Wired into the session at `libs/cli/src/server/session-manager.ts:597-598`.

**(c)** `db:schema`. Scoped in `libs/core/src/exec/app-globals.ts:155-165` (`addColumn` is table-scope-checked; `createTable` is not).
**(d) DTS** (`library-dts.ts:154-155`): `createTable(schema: any): void; addColumn(table: string, name: string, column: any): void;` — synchronous, on the `db` object.

**Does the automator hold `db:schema`?** **YES.** `libs/core/system-spaces/system-appbuilder/agents/automator/instruct.md:7-13`:
```yaml
capabilities:
  - hooks:write
  - db:schema
  - db:read
  - db:write
  - pages:write
  - api:write
```
So `db.addColumn` **is injected and is in its DTS**. Scenario 07 Act III is asserting a **real** feature.

**Why it fails:** the automator's instruct.md **never mentions `db.addColumn` or `db.createTable` — not once** (verified: the only mentions of those symbols in all of `system-spaces/` are two *knowledge* capability tables, `system-appbuilder/knowledge/app_building/model/capability-model.md:13` and `system-architect/.../capabilities.md:29`). Instead it explicitly routes the migration case through the schema writer — instruct.md:400:

> "If the concept genuinely has no column yet, ADD one (`writeProjectTable` with the extra column)"

and instruct.md:257: "if a target row/column is missing, ADD it (insert a row, or `writeProjectTable` to add the column)".

That path *does* migrate additively and *does* preserve data — `writeProjectTable` merges (`mergeWithExistingTable`, `authoring/globals.ts:571-583`) → `onSchemaWrite` → `reloadProjectDb` → `reconcileTable` issues `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN` (`libs/cli/src/app/boot.ts:153-166`). **So the product behaviour is correct; the Act's assertion (`/db\s*\.\s*addColumn\s*\(/` over statements, `scenarios/07-life-admin/run.mjs:536-548`) is testing a path the shipped instructions steer the agent AWAY from.** Either the automator's instruct must teach `db.addColumn` for live column adds, or Act III must accept the `writeProjectTable`-merge path (which the run.mjs currently treats as the failure mode: `rewroteTable`).

**(e) Harness observation:** trace `statement` events (`turn().statements`) for the literal call; data survival via `GET /api/projects/:id/app/data/:table`; the new column via `GET /api/fs/read?path=<project>/database/<t>.json` (present under either path).

---

# 4. Space `components/form/<Name>.tsx` + `components/view/<Name>.tsx` — REAL runtime, PARTIAL rendering

**(a) Exists** at the runtime/descriptor level.
**(b)** Loaded by `loadComponents` (`/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/core/src/spaces/load.ts:208-240`; `view/` and `form/` dirs, one `<Name>.tsx` each). Per-agent filter: `libs/core/src/spaces/components.ts` (`getAgentComponents`).

**(c) Opt-in = the per-agent frontmatter key `components:`** (`load.ts:475`), NOT a capability. A component named there but absent on disk **fails the space load** (`load.ts:693-697`: `Agent "X" requires component "Y" but it was not found in components/view or components/form`). Every shipped system agent has `components: []` today — there is **no shipped example**; a scenario must author one.

**(d) To the model:** injected as a stub whose `displayName` is the name (`libs/core/src/exec/bootstrap.ts:230-258`), and the React shim collapses JSX to a plain descriptor whose `type` is the **component name string**:
```ts
const typeName = typeof type === 'string' ? type
  : type && typeof type === 'object' && 'displayName' in type ? type.displayName : String(type);
return { type: typeName, props: props ?? {}, children: [...] };
```
DTS: `buildOverlay` emits `declare function <Name>(props: <Name>Props): JSXDescriptor;` (`libs/core/src/typecheck/overlay.ts:81-91`).

**(e) Harness:** `GET /api/sessions/:id/asks?format=json` → `{ asks: [{ id, descriptor }] }`, and `descriptor.type === '<Name>'`. `thing.mjs`'s `onAsk(descriptor, ask)` receives exactly that. **So a harness CAN assert the component name.**

⚠️ **Caveat you must not assert:** actual browser rendering of a space form component is bundled only by the legacy `--web` DevTools server (`libs/cli/src/web/serve.ts:80-106`, `window.__SPACE_COMPONENTS__`). **`apps/web` (the pod SPA) never sets `__SPACE_COMPONENTS__`** — `libs/ui/src/chat/app/Message.tsx:19-21` falls back to `CatalogForm`/generic descriptor rendering. Assert the descriptor, not the pixels.

---

# 5. `ask()` security guards — REAL

**(b)** `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/core/src/globals/ask.ts:8-57`:

```ts
const BLOCKED_TYPES = new Set(['script','iframe','object','embed','frame','frameset']);
if (BLOCKED_TYPES.has(type)) throw new Error(`ask(): blocked descriptor type "${type}"`);
if (desc.props['dangerouslySetInnerHTML'] !== undefined) throw new Error('ask(): dangerouslySetInnerHTML is not allowed');
if (typeof val === 'string' && val.trim().toLowerCase().startsWith('javascript:'))
  throw new Error(`ask(): javascript: URL not allowed in prop "${key}"`);
```
Recursive over `children`. A non-descriptor arg → `ask(): argument must be a JSX descriptor`.

**(c)** None — `ask` is universal (gated only by context: absent in forks/delegates).
**Behaviour:** the yield is **never pushed**; `ask()` returns a **rejected Promise** (`Promise.reject(err)`), so the statement throws inside the VM.
**(e) Harness:** it surfaces as a trace **`eval_error`** event (`turn().errors` in `thing.mjs:380`) with that exact message, and **no** `ask_start` / no entry in `GET /api/sessions/:id/asks`. Assert both.

---

# 6. Cancelled `ask()` resolves `null` — REAL

**(b)** `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/rpc/server.ts:54-60`:
```ts
cancelAsk(id: string): void {
  const resolver = this.askResolvers.get(id);
  if (resolver) { this.askResolvers.delete(id); resolver(null); }
}
```
**The agent sees `null`** — the `ask()` promise **resolves** with `null`, it does not reject. (`submitForm` resolves with the posted value; `ask_end` is emitted either way.)

**HTTP surface:** `DELETE /api/sessions/:id/ask/:askId` → `{ ok: true }` (`libs/cli/src/web/agent-api.ts:321-336`).

⚠️ **`onAsk` returning `null` does NOT cancel.** `scenarios/harness/lib/thing.mjs:315-319`:
```js
const answer = this.onAsk ? this.onAsk(ask.descriptor, ask) : undefined;
if (answer !== undefined) { await this.answerAsk(ask.id, answer).catch(() => {}); }
```
`answerAsk` does `POST /api/sessions/:id/ask/:askId {value: null}` → `submitForm(id, null)` → the agent gets `null` **via the submit path**, not the cancel path. Returning `undefined` leaves the ask **open** (turn stalls to timeout). **There is no `cancelAsk` helper in `thing.mjs` today** — to exercise the true cancel path an Act must call `pod.req('DELETE', '/api/sessions/'+id+'/ask/'+askId)` directly. Both paths land `null` in the agent; only the DELETE path is the real "cancelled" semantic.

---

# 7. App API handler that THROWS — REAL worker crash boundary

**(b) Executed in a `node:worker_threads` worker** — bundled `worker.ts` (`libs/cli/src/app/api/worker.ts`), spawned per request by `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/app/api/runtime.ts`. Catch boundary at `runtime.ts:300-327`:

```ts
} else if (msg.type === 'error') {
  if (msg.serialized) { settle(errorResponseFor(msg.serialized)); }
  else { log(`[api] handler error: ${msg.message ?? 'unknown'}`); settle({ status: 500, body: toErrorBody(500, 'internal error') }); }
}
worker.on('error', (err) => { log('[api] worker error', err); settle({status:500, body: toErrorBody(500,'internal error')}); });
worker.on('exit', (exitCode) => { if (settled) return; settle({status:500, body: toErrorBody(500,'internal error')}); });
```
**The pod survives** — that is the explicit contract ("Worker CRASH isolation — an uncaught throw or a non-zero exit (`process.exit(1)`, segfault) must NOT take down the main process").

**HttpError type:** `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/app/api/errors.ts` — `class HttpError extends Error { status; details? }`, importable in a handler as `import { HttpError } from '@app/runtime'` (`libs/cli/src/app/api/handler-module.ts:32`). Crosses the thread as `{ __httpError: true, status, message, details }` (`serializeHttpError`), reconstructed by `errorResponseFor`.

**Client gets:**
- `throw new HttpError(409, 'msg', d)` → **status 409**, body `{ error: { status: 409, message: 'msg', details: d } }`.
- Any other throw / crash / `process.exit(1)` → **status 500**, body `{ error: { status: 500, message: 'internal error' } }` — the real message is **deliberately never leaked**.
- ajv input mismatch → **400** `{ error: { status:400, message:'invalid input', details } }` (`validationErrorBody`).

**(e) Harness:** `pod.appApi(projectId, route, body)` (`scenarios/harness/lib/pod.mjs:176`), then a subsequent healthy call to prove the pod is alive.
**(c) Capability:** none for the caller (it's HTTP); `api:write` to author the handler.

---

# 8. History summarization — REAL, but **INVISIBLE to an HTTP harness**

**(b)** `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/core/src/session/session.ts:541-549`:
```ts
private async maybeSummarizeHistory(): Promise<void> {
  const maxTurns = this.opts.maxHistoryTurns;
  if (!maxTurns || this.history.messages.length <= maxTurns * 2) return;
  const summary = await summarizeHistory({ messages: this.history.messages, keepLast: 6 });
  if (summary) {
    this.history.summarize(summary, 6);
    this.opts.renderHost.log(`[context] history summarized → ${this.history.messages.length} messages`);
  }
}
```
Trigger is `messages.length > maxHistoryTurns * 2`, keeps the last 6 verbatim. It is a **deterministic digest, no extra LLM call** (`libs/core/src/context/summarize.ts`).

**(c)** Config: `SessionOpts.maxHistoryTurns` (`libs/core/src/session/types.ts:36-38`). **Pod default = `20`** — hard-coded at `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/server/session-manager.ts:413`: `maxHistoryTurns: 20,` (⇒ fires above **40 messages**). Not settable via `POST /api/sessions`.

**(e) ❌ NOT OBSERVABLE over HTTP.** There is **no summarization TraceEvent** — the `TraceEvent` union (`libs/core/src/sandbox/trace.ts:57-86`) has no such member, and the only signal is `renderHost.log(...)`, which goes to **stdout only** (`libs/cli/src/rpc/server.ts:66-72`: *"Turn-loop debug chatter — goes to the server console, NOT the browser conversation"*). `GET /api/sessions/:id/events` serves the **TraceHub**, which never sees it.

**Verdict: an Act asserting "the harness sees a summarization event" is a FANTASY today.** Indirect proxies only: `{type:'llm_request'}.messages.length` collapsing across turns (the trace *does* carry full `messages` on `llm_request`), or a `system`/`user` message whose content is the digest. If you want a real assertion, the product needs a new trace event (e.g. `{type:'context_summarized', kept, total}`) — that would be a legitimate product ask.

---

# 9. `readDocument` on an image — REAL, but it **does NOT throw**

**(b)** Global: `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/core/src/globals/read-document.ts` — yield kind `'readDocument'`. Resolver: `resolveUploadDocument` in `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/server/uploads.ts:290`, wired at `session-manager.ts:423`.

**Behaviour on an image:** it returns a **structured result, not an exception** (`ReadDocumentResult`, read-document.ts:10-22):
```ts
{ ok, attachmentId, mediaType, filename?, kind: 'text' | 'unsupported', text?, truncated?, error? }
```
An image (`classifyKind` → `'image'`, `uploads.ts:38-42`) yields `kind: 'unsupported'` with a human-readable `error`. `extractDocumentText` only handles `application/pdf` via `unpdf` (`uploads.ts:57-69`); a **scanned** PDF has no text layer and is rasterized into per-page **image** uploads (`UploadMeta.pages`) precisely so it can flow down the image→vision path (uploads.ts:23-29).

**(c) Capability: NONE — injected universally** (like `fetch`), per the doc comment in `read-document.ts`. It is *not* gated.

**Delegation to system-vision is NOT automatic** — it is instruction-driven. `system-vision/agents/vision` exists; whether THING routes there depends on `user-thing/thing`'s instruct + its `canDelegateTo`. So an Act may assert (i) the `readDocument` yield resolving to `kind:'unsupported'`, and (ii) a subsequent `{type:'yield', kind:'delegate'}` naming `system-vision` — but the second is a *model-behaviour* assertion, not a runtime guarantee.

**(e) Harness:** `turn().yields` → `{kind:'readDocument'}` + the `yield_resolved` value carrying `kind:'unsupported'`; `didDelegateTo('system-vision')` (`thing.mjs:399-403`).

---

## Bottom line for your Acts

| # | Capability | Real? | Watch out |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `apiCall` | ✅ yield `apiCall`, allowlist-enforced | **Only THING has the grant.** No appbuilder agent can call it. |
| 2 | `include` relations | ✅ in `db.query` / `ctx.db.query` | **Admin `app/data/:table` API does NOT expand.** Assert through an authored API route. |
| 3 | `db.createTable` / `db.addColumn` | ✅ API + `db:schema` granted to automator | **Instructions never teach it** — they route to `writeProjectTable`. This is the bug. |
| 4 | space `form/`+`view/` components | ✅ descriptor + DTS; opt-in `components:` frontmatter | Rendering only in legacy `--web`; **assert `descriptor.type === '<Name>'` via `/asks`.** |
| 5 | `ask()` guards | ✅ | Rejects the promise → `eval_error`; no ask ever opens. |
| 6 | cancelled `ask()` → `null` | ✅ | `onAsk` returning `null` **submits** null; true cancel = `DELETE …/ask/:id` (no harness helper yet). |
| 7 | handler throw / `HttpError` | ✅ worker-isolated | Non-`HttpError` → **500 `internal error`**, message never leaked; pod survives. |
| 8 | history summarization | ⚠️ **runs, but emits NO event** | **Not observable over HTTP.** Default `maxHistoryTurns: 20`. An Act here would be a fantasy. |
| 9 | `readDocument` on image | ✅ but **returns `{kind:'unsupported', error}`, does not throw** | Vision delegation is instruction-driven, not a runtime fallback. |

content.text:
I have what I need. Here is the precise, per-capability report.

---

# 1. `apiCall(name, input)` — REAL, but only THING holds the grant

**(a) Exists.** **(b)** Global factory: `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/core/src/globals/api-call.ts` (`createApiCallGlobal`). It is a **YIELD**, kind `'apiCall'`:

```ts
pushYield({ kind: 'apiCall', args: [name, input], deferred: { resolve, reject }, vmPromiseHandle: undefined });
```

Routed in `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/core/src/eval/yield-router.ts:190-210`. Kind is in the union at `libs/core/src/eval/yield.ts:4`. Host resolver: `libs/cli/src/app/api/runtime.ts` (`callByName`) — re-enters the *same* main-process api runtime as the browser's `useApi`.

**(c) Capability:** `api:call` — and it is **allowlist-gated, and the allowlist is REQUIRED** (a bare `api:call` fails the space load; `libs/core/src/spaces/capabilities.ts`). Enforcement is in the router, not at injection:

```ts
const allow = ctx.apiCallAllow;
if (allow && !allow.includes('*') && !allow.includes(name)) {
  throw new Error(`apiCall("${name}") is not permitted: this agent's api:call allowlist is [${allow.join(', ')}]`);
}
```

**(d) DTS:** `libs/core/src/typecheck/library-dts.ts:175`
`declare function apiCall(name: string, input?: unknown): Promise<any>;` — replaced by generated per-endpoint overloads when the project supplies `appDts`.

**(e) Harness observation (HTTP only):** `GET /api/sessions/:id/events?format=json` returns **TraceEvents**; you will see `{type:'yield', kind:'apiCall', args:[name, input]}` and a matching `{type:'yield_resolved', kind:'apiCall', value}`. `scenarios/harness/lib/thing.mjs` already exposes `didYield('apiCall')` and `turn().yields`. It is **not** merely a `statement` event.

**Who holds it today:** ONLY `user-thing/thing` — `libs/core/system-spaces/user-thing/agents/thing/instruct.md:9`: `- api:call: { allow: ['*'] }`. **The automator, app-architect, api-author, page-builder, data-modeler hold NO `api:call`.** An Act asserting the *automator* calling `apiCall` is a fantasy; asserting THING doing it is real.

**Docs page:** `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/org/docs/runtime-globals/app-authoring.md:127` (`## apiCall(name, input?) → Promise<any> — api:call`) plus `data-db.md:174`, `README.md:251`.

---

# 2. `db.query` `include` — REAL for agents/handlers; NOT exposed by the admin data API

**(a) Exists.** **(b)** `QueryOpts.include?: string[]` (`libs/core/src/db/types.ts:23`); expansion in `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/app/store.ts:473-506` (`expandIncludes` — `belongsTo` → object, `hasMany` → array).

**Declaration shape** (`database/<table>.json`, `libs/core/src/db/schema.ts:61-107`):

```json
{
  "title": "Orders",
  "description": "One order the user placed.",
  "columns": {
    "id": { "type": "string", "description": "PK", "primaryKey": true, "generated": "uuid" }
  },
  "relations": {
    "items":  { "hasMany": "order_items", "via": "order_id", "description": "the lines on this order" },
    "parent": { "belongsTo": "orders",    "via": "order_id", "description": "the order this line is on" }
  }
}
```
`description` on a relation is **required** (`libs/core/src/db/validate.ts:71-78`).

**(c)** `db:read` (query lives in `DB_READ_MEMBERS`). **(d) DTS** (`library-dts.ts:154`): `query(table, opts?: { where?; include?: string[]; orderBy?; limit?; offset? }): any[]`.

**Writer accepts relations?** YES — `writeProjectTable` calls `validateTableSchema(name, schema)` (`libs/cli/src/app/authoring/globals.ts:605`), which validates `relations` (description only, single-table). The automator is *explicitly taught* the relation shape: `libs/core/system-spaces/system-appbuilder/agents/automator/instruct.md:198-230` ("DECLARE THE RELATION…").
⚠️ **Sharp edge:** cross-table resolution (`relation targets unknown table "X"`) only runs at *set* validation (`validate.ts:154-162`, via `libs/cli/src/app/loader.ts:97`) — a relation to a table that doesn't exist yet passes the writer and fails the whole project db at boot.

**(e) Harness observation:** **The served admin data API does NOT expand relations.** `handleListRows` (`/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/server/routes/app-admin.ts:344-377`) passes only paging:
```ts
const rows = pdb.db.query(table, { limit, offset });
```
So `GET /api/projects/:id/app/data/:table` gives you flat rows. To observe an expansion over HTTP you must go through a **project API handler** the agent authored (`ctx.db.query(t, { include: [...] })`) and hit it via `pod.appApi(projectId, route, body)`. Alternatively assert the *declaration* by `GET /api/fs/read?path=<project>/database/<t>.json` and check `.relations`.

---

# 3. `db.createTable` / `db.addColumn` — REAL API, GRANTED to the automator, but the instructions never teach it → **this is the round's product bug**

**(a) Exists, live DDL.** **(b)** `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/app/store.ts:510-524`:

```ts
function createTable(schema: TableSchema): void {
  const name = slugifyTitle(schema.title);
  raw.exec(schemaToCreateTableSql(name, schema));
  registry.set(name, cloneSchema(schema));
}
function addColumn(table: string, name: string, column: ColumnSchema): void {
  raw.exec(`ALTER TABLE ${ident(table)} ADD COLUMN ${columnDefSql(name, column, true)}`);
  const schema = registry.get(table);
  if (schema) schema.columns[name] = column;
}
```
Wired into the session at `libs/cli/src/server/session-manager.ts:597-598`.

**(c)** `db:schema`. Scoped in `libs/core/src/exec/app-globals.ts:155-165` (`addColumn` is table-scope-checked; `createTable` is not).
**(d) DTS** (`library-dts.ts:154-155`): `createTable(schema: any): void; addColumn(table: string, name: string, column: any): void;` — synchronous, on the `db` object.

**Does the automator hold `db:schema`?** **YES.** `libs/core/system-spaces/system-appbuilder/agents/automator/instruct.md:7-13`:
```yaml
capabilities:
  - hooks:write
  - db:schema
  - db:read
  - db:write
  - pages:write
  - api:write
```
So `db.addColumn` **is injected and is in its DTS**. Scenario 07 Act III is asserting a **real** feature.

**Why it fails:** the automator's instruct.md **never mentions `db.addColumn` or `db.createTable` — not once** (verified: the only mentions of those symbols in all of `system-spaces/` are two *knowledge* capability tables, `system-appbuilder/knowledge/app_building/model/capability-model.md:13` and `system-architect/.../capabilities.md:29`). Instead it explicitly routes the migration case through the schema writer — instruct.md:400:

> "If the concept genuinely has no column yet, ADD one (`writeProjectTable` with the extra column)"

and instruct.md:257: "if a target row/column is missing, ADD it (insert a row, or `writeProjectTable` to add the column)".

That path *does* migrate additively and *does* preserve data — `writeProjectTable` merges (`mergeWithExistingTable`, `authoring/globals.ts:571-583`) → `onSchemaWrite` → `reloadProjectDb` → `reconcileTable` issues `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN` (`libs/cli/src/app/boot.ts:153-166`). **So the product behaviour is correct; the Act's assertion (`/db\s*\.\s*addColumn\s*\(/` over statements, `scenarios/07-life-admin/run.mjs:536-548`) is testing a path the shipped instructions steer the agent AWAY from.** Either the automator's instruct must teach `db.addColumn` for live column adds, or Act III must accept the `writeProjectTable`-merge path (which the run.mjs currently treats as the failure mode: `rewroteTable`).

**(e) Harness observation:** trace `statement` events (`turn().statements`) for the literal call; data survival via `GET /api/projects/:id/app/data/:table`; the new column via `GET /api/fs/read?path=<project>/database/<t>.json` (present under either path).

---

# 4. Space `components/form/<Name>.tsx` + `components/view/<Name>.tsx` — REAL runtime, PARTIAL rendering

**(a) Exists** at the runtime/descriptor level.
**(b)** Loaded by `loadComponents` (`/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/core/src/spaces/load.ts:208-240`; `view/` and `form/` dirs, one `<Name>.tsx` each). Per-agent filter: `libs/core/src/spaces/components.ts` (`getAgentComponents`).

**(c) Opt-in = the per-agent frontmatter key `components:`** (`load.ts:475`), NOT a capability. A component named there but absent on disk **fails the space load** (`load.ts:693-697`: `Agent "X" requires component "Y" but it was not found in components/view or components/form`). Every shipped system agent has `components: []` today — there is **no shipped example**; a scenario must author one.

**(d) To the model:** injected as a stub whose `displayName` is the name (`libs/core/src/exec/bootstrap.ts:230-258`), and the React shim collapses JSX to a plain descriptor whose `type` is the **component name string**:
```ts
const typeName = typeof type === 'string' ? type
  : type && typeof type === 'object' && 'displayName' in type ? type.displayName : String(type);
return { type: typeName, props: props ?? {}, children: [...] };
```
DTS: `buildOverlay` emits `declare function <Name>(props: <Name>Props): JSXDescriptor;` (`libs/core/src/typecheck/overlay.ts:81-91`).

**(e) Harness:** `GET /api/sessions/:id/asks?format=json` → `{ asks: [{ id, descriptor }] }`, and `descriptor.type === '<Name>'`. `thing.mjs`'s `onAsk(descriptor, ask)` receives exactly that. **So a harness CAN assert the component name.**

⚠️ **Caveat you must not assert:** actual browser rendering of a space form component is bundled only by the legacy `--web` DevTools server (`libs/cli/src/web/serve.ts:80-106`, `window.__SPACE_COMPONENTS__`). **`apps/web` (the pod SPA) never sets `__SPACE_COMPONENTS__`** — `libs/ui/src/chat/app/Message.tsx:19-21` falls back to `CatalogForm`/generic descriptor rendering. Assert the descriptor, not the pixels.

---

# 5. `ask()` security guards — REAL

**(b)** `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/core/src/globals/ask.ts:8-57`:

```ts
const BLOCKED_TYPES = new Set(['script','iframe','object','embed','frame','frameset']);
if (BLOCKED_TYPES.has(type)) throw new Error(`ask(): blocked descriptor type "${type}"`);
if (desc.props['dangerouslySetInnerHTML'] !== undefined) throw new Error('ask(): dangerouslySetInnerHTML is not allowed');
if (typeof val === 'string' && val.trim().toLowerCase().startsWith('javascript:'))
  throw new Error(`ask(): javascript: URL not allowed in prop "${key}"`);
```
Recursive over `children`. A non-descriptor arg → `ask(): argument must be a JSX descriptor`.

**(c)** None — `ask` is universal (gated only by context: absent in forks/delegates).
**Behaviour:** the yield is **never pushed**; `ask()` returns a **rejected Promise** (`Promise.reject(err)`), so the statement throws inside the VM.
**(e) Harness:** it surfaces as a trace **`eval_error`** event (`turn().errors` in `thing.mjs:380`) with that exact message, and **no** `ask_start` / no entry in `GET /api/sessions/:id/asks`. Assert both.

---

# 6. Cancelled `ask()` resolves `null` — REAL

**(b)** `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/rpc/server.ts:54-60`:
```ts
cancelAsk(id: string): void {
  const resolver = this.askResolvers.get(id);
  if (resolver) { this.askResolvers.delete(id); resolver(null); }
}
```
**The agent sees `null`** — the `ask()` promise **resolves** with `null`, it does not reject. (`submitForm` resolves with the posted value; `ask_end` is emitted either way.)

**HTTP surface:** `DELETE /api/sessions/:id/ask/:askId` → `{ ok: true }` (`libs/cli/src/web/agent-api.ts:321-336`).

⚠️ **`onAsk` returning `null` does NOT cancel.** `scenarios/harness/lib/thing.mjs:315-319`:
```js
const answer = this.onAsk ? this.onAsk(ask.descriptor, ask) : undefined;
if (answer !== undefined) { await this.answerAsk(ask.id, answer).catch(() => {}); }
```
`answerAsk` does `POST /api/sessions/:id/ask/:askId {value: null}` → `submitForm(id, null)` → the agent gets `null` **via the submit path**, not the cancel path. Returning `undefined` leaves the ask **open** (turn stalls to timeout). **There is no `cancelAsk` helper in `thing.mjs` today** — to exercise the true cancel path an Act must call `pod.req('DELETE', '/api/sessions/'+id+'/ask/'+askId)` directly. Both paths land `null` in the agent; only the DELETE path is the real "cancelled" semantic.

---

# 7. App API handler that THROWS — REAL worker crash boundary

**(b) Executed in a `node:worker_threads` worker** — bundled `worker.ts` (`libs/cli/src/app/api/worker.ts`), spawned per request by `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/app/api/runtime.ts`. Catch boundary at `runtime.ts:300-327`:

```ts
} else if (msg.type === 'error') {
  if (msg.serialized) { settle(errorResponseFor(msg.serialized)); }
  else { log(`[api] handler error: ${msg.message ?? 'unknown'}`); settle({ status: 500, body: toErrorBody(500, 'internal error') }); }
}
worker.on('error', (err) => { log('[api] worker error', err); settle({status:500, body: toErrorBody(500,'internal error')}); });
worker.on('exit', (exitCode) => { if (settled) return; settle({status:500, body: toErrorBody(500,'internal error')}); });
```
**The pod survives** — that is the explicit contract ("Worker CRASH isolation — an uncaught throw or a non-zero exit (`process.exit(1)`, segfault) must NOT take down the main process").

**HttpError type:** `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/app/api/errors.ts` — `class HttpError extends Error { status; details? }`, importable in a handler as `import { HttpError } from '@app/runtime'` (`libs/cli/src/app/api/handler-module.ts:32`). Crosses the thread as `{ __httpError: true, status, message, details }` (`serializeHttpError`), reconstructed by `errorResponseFor`.

**Client gets:**
- `throw new HttpError(409, 'msg', d)` → **status 409**, body `{ error: { status: 409, message: 'msg', details: d } }`.
- Any other throw / crash / `process.exit(1)` → **status 500**, body `{ error: { status: 500, message: 'internal error' } }` — the real message is **deliberately never leaked**.
- ajv input mismatch → **400** `{ error: { status:400, message:'invalid input', details } }` (`validationErrorBody`).

**(e) Harness:** `pod.appApi(projectId, route, body)` (`scenarios/harness/lib/pod.mjs:176`), then a subsequent healthy call to prove the pod is alive.
**(c) Capability:** none for the caller (it's HTTP); `api:write` to author the handler.

---

# 8. History summarization — REAL, but **INVISIBLE to an HTTP harness**

**(b)** `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/core/src/session/session.ts:541-549`:
```ts

  const maxTurns = this.opts.maxHistoryTurns;

If you want a real assertion, the product needs a new trace event (e.g. `{type:'context_summarized', kept, total}`) — that would be a legitimate product ask.

---

**(b)** Global: `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/core/src/globals/read-document.ts` — yield kind `'readDocument'`. Resolver: `resolveUploadDocument` in `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/server/uploads.ts:290`, wired at `session-manager.ts:423`.

```

tool_use_result.prompt:
READ-ONLY investigation in /home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing (the sdk/org submodule holds the runtime). I am writing scenario Acts that must assert REAL product surface. For EACH capability below, tell me: (a) does it exist today, (b) the exact source symbol/file, (c) the capability grant it needs (`capabilities:` frontmatter value), (d) how it appears in the DTS / to the model, (e) how an external harness (HTTP only, via the pod REST API) could OBSERVE it happened. Be precise and quote code.

1. **`apiCall(name, input)`** — an agent calling the project's OWN api endpoint. Where is it implemented (libs/core/src/exec/app-globals.ts? globals?), what capability gates it, is it allowlist-gated, does it appear as a YIELD (kind name?) or as an injected host fn (i.e. only visible in `statement` events)? Which agents (system-appbuilder/automator? thing?) actually have the grant today? Check org/docs/runtime-globals for its page.

content.text:
I have what I need. Here is the precise, per-capability report.

---

# 1. `apiCall(name, input)` — REAL, but only THING holds the grant

**(a) Exists.** **(b)** Global factory: `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/core/src/globals/api-call.ts` (`createApiCallGlobal`). It is a **YIELD**, kind `'apiCall'`:

```ts
pushYield({ kind: 'apiCall', args: [name, input], deferred: { resolve, reject }, vmPromiseHandle: undefined });
```

Routed in `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/core/src/eval/yield-router.ts:190-210`. Kind is in the union at `libs/core/src/eval/yield.ts:4`. Host resolver: `libs/cli/src/app/api/runtime.ts` (`callByName`) — re-enters the *same* main-process api runtime as the browser's `useApi`.

**(c) Capability:** `api:call` — and it is **allowlist-gated, and the allowlist is REQUIRED** (a bare `api:call` fails the space load; `libs/core/src/spaces/capabilities.ts`). Enforcement is in the router, not at injection:

```ts
const allow = ctx.apiCallAllow;
if (allow && !allow.includes('*') && !allow.includes(name)) {
  throw new Error(`apiCall("${name}") is not permitted: this agent's api:call allowlist is [${allow.join(', ')}]`);
}
```

**(d) DTS:** `libs/core/src/typecheck/library-dts.ts:175`
`declare function apiCall(name: string, input?: unknown): Promise<any>;` — replaced by generated per-endpoint overloads when the project supplies `appDts`.

**(e) Harness observation (HTTP only):** `GET /api/sessions/:id/events?format=json` returns **TraceEvents**; you will see `{type:'yield', kind:'apiCall', args:[name, input]}` and a matching `{type:'yield_resolved', kind:'apiCall', value}`. `scenarios/harness/lib/thing.mjs` already exposes `didYield('apiCall')` and `turn().yields`. It is **not** merely a `statement` event.

**Who holds it today:** ONLY `user-thing/thing` — `libs/core/system-spaces/user-thing/agents/thing/instruct.md:9`: `- api:call: { allow: ['*'] }`. **The automator, app-architect, api-author, page-builder, data-modeler hold NO `api:call`.** An Act asserting the *automator* calling `apiCall` is a fantasy; asserting THING doing it is real.

**Docs page:** `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/org/docs/runtime-globals/app-authoring.md:127` (`## apiCall(name, input?) → Promise<any> — api:call`) plus `data-db.md:174`, `README.md:251`.

---

# 2. `db.query` `include` — REAL for agents/handlers; NOT exposed by the admin data API

**(a) Exists.** **(b)** `QueryOpts.include?: string[]` (`libs/core/src/db/types.ts:23`); expansion in `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/app/store.ts:473-506` (`expandIncludes` — `belongsTo` → object, `hasMany` → array).

**Declaration shape** (`database/<table>.json`, `libs/core/src/db/schema.ts:61-107`):

```json
{
  "title": "Orders",
  "description": "One order the user placed.",
  "columns": {
    "id": { "type": "string", "description": "PK", "primaryKey": true, "generated": "uuid" }
  },
  "relations": {
    "items":  { "hasMany": "order_items", "via": "order_id", "description": "the lines on this order" },
    "parent": { "belongsTo": "orders",    "via": "order_id", "description": "the order this line is on" }
  }
}
```
`description` on a relation is **required** (`libs/core/src/db/validate.ts:71-78`).

**(c)** `db:read` (query lives in `DB_READ_MEMBERS`). **(d) DTS** (`library-dts.ts:154`): `query(table, opts?: { where?; include?: string[]; orderBy?; limit?; offset? }): any[]`.

**Writer accepts relations?** YES — `writeProjectTable` calls `validateTableSchema(name, schema)` (`libs/cli/src/app/authoring/globals.ts:605`), which validates `relations` (description only, single-table). The automator is *explicitly taught* the relation shape: `libs/core/system-spaces/system-appbuilder/agents/automator/instruct.md:198-230` ("DECLARE THE RELATION…").
⚠️ **Sharp edge:** cross-table resolution (`relation targets unknown table "X"`) only runs at *set* validation (`validate.ts:154-162`, via `libs/cli/src/app/loader.ts:97`) — a relation to a table that doesn't exist yet passes the writer and fails the whole project db at boot.

**(e) Harness observation:** **The served admin data API does NOT expand relations.** `handleListRows` (`/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/server/routes/app-admin.ts:344-377`) passes only paging:
```ts

```
So `GET /api/projects/:id/app/data/:table` gives you flat rows. To observe an expansion over HTTP you must go through a **project API handler** the agent authored (`ctx.db.query(t, { include: [...] })`) and hit it via `pod.appApi(projectId, route, body)`. Alternatively assert the *declaration* by `GET /api/fs/read?path=<project>/database/<t>.json` and check `.relations`.

---

# 3. `db.createTable` / `db.addColumn` — REAL API, GRANTED to the automator, but the instructions never teach it → **this is the round's product bug**

**(a) Exists, live DDL.** **(b)** `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/app/store.ts:510-524`:

```ts
function createTable(schema: TableSchema): void {
  const name = slugifyTitle(schema.title);
  raw.exec(schemaToCreateTableSql(name, schema));
  registry.set(name, cloneSchema(schema));
}
function addColumn(table: string, name: string, column: ColumnSchema): void {
  raw.exec(`ALTER TABLE ${ident(table)} ADD COLUMN ${columnDefSql(name, column, true)}`);
  const schema = registry.get(table);
  if (schema) schema.columns[name] = column;
}
```
Wired into the session at `libs/cli/src/server/session-manager.ts:597-598`.

**(c)** `db:schema`. Scoped in `libs/core/src/exec/app-globals.ts:155-165` (`addColumn` is table-scope-checked; `createTable` is not).
**(d) DTS** (`library-dts.ts:154-155`): `createTable(schema: any): void; addColumn(table: string, name: string, column: any): void;` — synchronous, on the `db` object.

**Does the automator hold `db:schema`?** **YES.** `libs/core/system-spaces/system-appbuilder/agents/automator/instruct.md:7-13`:
```yaml
capabilities:
  - hooks:write
  - db:schema
  - db:read
  - db:write
  - pages:write
  - api:write
```
So `db.addColumn` **is injected and is in its DTS**. Scenario 07 Act III is asserting a **real** feature.

**Why it fails:** the automator's instruct.md **never mentions `db.addColumn` or `db.createTable` — not once** (verified: the only mentions of those symbols in all of `system-spaces/` are two *knowledge* capability tables, `system-appbuilder/knowledge/app_building/model/capability-model.md:13` and `system-architect/.../capabilities.md:29`). Instead it explicitly routes the migration case through the schema writer — instruct.md:400:

> "If the concept genuinely has no column yet, ADD one (`writeProjectTable` with the extra column)"

and instruct.md:257: "if a target row/column is missing, ADD it (insert a row, or `writeProjectTable` to add the column)".

That path *does* migrate additively and *does* preserve data — `writeProjectTable` merges (`mergeWithExistingTable`, `authoring/globals.ts:571-583`) → `onSchemaWrite` → `reloadProjectDb` → `reconcileTable` issues `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN` (`libs/cli/src/app/boot.ts:153-166`). **So the product behaviour is correct; the Act's assertion (`/db\s*\.\s*addColumn\s*\(/` over statements, `scenarios/07-life-admin/run.mjs:536-548`) is testing a path the shipped instructions steer the agent AWAY from.** Either the automator's instruct must teach `db.addColumn` for live column adds, or Act III must accept the `writeProjectTable`-merge path (which the run.mjs currently treats as the failure mode: `rewroteTable`).

**(e) Harness observation:** trace `statement` events (`turn().statements`) for the literal call; data survival via `GET /api/projects/:id/app/data/:table`; the new column via `GET /api/fs/read?path=<project>/database/<t>.json` (present under either path).

---

# 4. Space `components/form/<Name>.tsx` + `components/view/<Name>.tsx` — REAL runtime, PARTIAL rendering

**(a) Exists** at the runtime/descriptor level.
**(b)** Loaded by `loadComponents` (`/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/core/src/spaces/load.ts:208-240`; `view/` and `form/` dirs, one `<Name>.tsx` each). Per-agent filter: `libs/core/src/spaces/components.ts` (`getAgentComponents`).

**(c) Opt-in = the per-agent frontmatter key `components:`** (`load.ts:475`), NOT a capability. A component named there but absent on disk **fails the space load** (`load.ts:693-697`: `Agent "X" requires component "Y" but it was not found in components/view or components/form`). Every shipped system agent has `components: []` today — there is **no shipped example**; a scenario must author one.

**(d) To the model:** injected as a stub whose `displayName` is the name (`libs/core/src/exec/bootstrap.ts:230-258`), and the React shim collapses JSX to a plain descriptor whose `type` is the **component name string**:
```ts

  : type && typeof type === 'object' && 'displayName' in type ? type.displayName : String(type);
return { type: typeName, props: props ?? {}, children: [...] };
```
DTS: `buildOverlay` emits `declare function <Name>(props: <Name>Props): JSXDescriptor;` (`libs/core/src/typecheck/overlay.ts:81-91`).

**(e) Harness:** `GET /api/sessions/:id/asks?format=json` → `{ asks: [{ id, descriptor }] }`, and `descriptor.type === '<Name>'`. `thing.mjs`'s `onAsk(descriptor, ask)` receives exactly that. **So a harness CAN assert the component name.**

⚠️ **Caveat you must not assert:** actual browser rendering of a space form component is bundled only by the legacy `--web` DevTools server (`libs/cli/src/web/serve.ts:80-106`, `window.__SPACE_COMPONENTS__`). **`apps/web` (the pod SPA) never sets `__SPACE_COMPONENTS__`** — `libs/ui/src/chat/app/Message.tsx:19-21` falls back to `CatalogForm`/generic descriptor rendering. Assert the descriptor, not the pixels.

---

# 5. `ask()` security guards — REAL

**(b)** `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/core/src/globals/ask.ts:8-57`:

```ts
const BLOCKED_TYPES = new Set(['script','iframe','object','embed','frame','frameset']);
if (BLOCKED_TYPES.has(type)) throw new Error(`ask(): blocked descriptor type "${type}"`);
if (desc.props['dangerouslySetInnerHTML'] !== undefined) throw new Error('ask(): dangerouslySetInnerHTML is not allowed');
if (typeof val === 'string' && val.trim().toLowerCase().startsWith('javascript:'))
  throw new Error(`ask(): javascript: URL not allowed in prop "${key}"`);
```
Recursive over `children`. A non-descriptor arg → `ask(): argument must be a JSX descriptor`.

**(c)** None — `ask` is universal (gated only by context: absent in forks/delegates).
**Behaviour:** the yield is **never pushed**; `ask()` returns a **rejected Promise** (`Promise.reject(err)`), so the statement throws inside the VM.
**(e) Harness:** it surfaces as a trace **`eval_error`** event (`turn().errors` in `thing.mjs:380`) with that exact message, and **no** `ask_start` / no entry in `GET /api/sessions/:id/asks`. Assert both.

---

# 6. Cancelled `ask()` resolves `null` — REAL

**(b)** `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/rpc/server.ts:54-60`:
```ts
cancelAsk(id: string): void {
  const resolver = this.askResolvers.get(id);
  if (resolver) { this.askResolvers.delete(id); resolver(null); }
}
```
**The agent sees `null`** — the `ask()` promise **resolves** with `null`, it does not reject. (`submitForm` resolves with the posted value; `ask_end` is emitted either way.)

**HTTP surface:** `DELETE /api/sessions/:id/ask/:askId` → `{ ok: true }` (`libs/cli/src/web/agent-api.ts:321-336`).

⚠️ **`onAsk` returning `null` does NOT cancel.** `scenarios/harness/lib/thing.mjs:315-319`:
```js
const answer = this.onAsk ? this.onAsk(ask.descriptor, ask) : undefined;
if (answer !== undefined) { await this.answerAsk(ask.id, answer).catch(() => {}); }
```
`answerAsk` does `POST /api/sessions/:id/ask/:askId {value: null}` → `submitForm(id, null)` → the agent gets `null` **via the submit path**, not the cancel path. Returning `undefined` leaves the ask **open** (turn stalls to timeout). **There is no `cancelAsk` helper in `thing.mjs` today** — to exercise the true cancel path an Act must call `pod.req('DELETE', '/api/sessions/'+id+'/ask/'+askId)` directly. Both paths land `null` in the agent; only the DELETE path is the real "cancelled" semantic.

---

# 7. App API handler that THROWS — REAL worker crash boundary

**(b) Executed in a `node:worker_threads` worker** — bundled `worker.ts` (`libs/cli/src/app/api/worker.ts`), spawned per request by `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/app/api/runtime.ts`. Catch boundary at `runtime.ts:300-327`:

```ts
} else if (msg.type === 'error') {
  if (msg.serialized) { settle(errorResponseFor(msg.serialized)); }
  else { log(`[api] handler error: ${msg.message ?? 'unknown'}`); settle({ status: 500, body: toErrorBody(500, 'internal error') }); }
}
worker.on('error', (err) => { log('[api] worker error', err); settle({status:500, body: toErrorBody(500,'internal error')}); });
worker.on('exit', (exitCode) => { if (settled) return; settle({status:500, body: toErrorBody(500,'internal error')}); });
```
**The pod survives** — that is the explicit contract ("Worker CRASH isolation — an uncaught throw or a non-zero exit (`process.exit(1)`, segfault) must NOT take down the main process").

**HttpError type:** `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/app/api/errors.ts` — `class HttpError extends Error { status; details? }`, importable in a handler as `import { HttpError } from '@app/runtime'` (`libs/cli/src/app/api/handler-module.ts:32`). Crosses the thread as `{ __httpError: true, status, message, details }` (`serializeHttpError`), reconstructed by `errorResponseFor`.

**Client gets:**
- `throw new HttpError(409, 'msg', d)` → **status 409**, body `{ error: { status: 409, message: 'msg', details: d } }`.
- Any other throw / crash / `process.exit(1)` → **status 500**, body `{ error: { status: 500, message: 'internal error' } }` — the real message is **deliberately never leaked**.
- ajv input mismatch → **400** `{ error: { status:400, message:'invalid input', details } }` (`validationErrorBody`).

**(e) Harness:** `pod.appApi(projectId, route, body)` (`scenarios/harness/lib/pod.mjs:176`), then a subsequent healthy call to prove the pod is alive.
**(c) Capability:** none for the caller (it's HTTP); `api:write` to author the handler.

---

# 8. History summarization — REAL, but **INVISIBLE to an HTTP harness**

**(b)** `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/core/src/session/session.ts:541-549`:
```ts
private async maybeSummarizeHistory(): Promise<void> {
  const maxTurns = this.opts.maxHistoryTurns;
  if (!maxTurns || this.history.messages.length <= maxTurns * 2) return;
  const summary = await summarizeHistory({ messages: this.history.messages, keepLast: 6 });
  if (summary) {
    this.history.summarize(summary, 6);
    this.opts.renderHost.log(`[context] history summarized → ${this.history.messages.length} messages`);
  }
}
```
Trigger is `messages.length > maxHistoryTurns * 2`, keeps the last 6 verbatim. It is a **deterministic digest, no extra LLM call** (`libs/core/src/context/summarize.ts`).

**(c)** Config: `SessionOpts.maxHistoryTurns` (`libs/core/src/session/types.ts:36-38`). **Pod default = `20`** — hard-coded at `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/server/session-manager.ts:413`: `maxHistoryTurns: 20,` (⇒ fires above **40 messages**). Not settable via `POST /api/sessions`.

**(e) ❌ NOT OBSERVABLE over HTTP.** There is **no summarization TraceEvent** — the `TraceEvent` union (`libs/core/src/sandbox/trace.ts:57-86`) has no such member, and the only signal is `renderHost.log(...)`, which goes to **stdout only** (`libs/cli/src/rpc/server.ts:66-72`: *"Turn-loop debug chatter — goes to the server console, NOT the browser conversation"*). `GET /api/sessions/:id/events` serves the **TraceHub**, which never sees it.

**Verdict: an Act asserting "the harness sees a summarization event" is a FANTASY today.** Indirect proxies only: `{type:'llm_request'}.messages.length` collapsing across turns (the trace *does* carry full `messages` on `llm_request`), or a `system`/`user` message whose content is the digest. If you want a real assertion, the product needs a new trace event (e.g. `{type:'context_summarized', kept, total}`) — that would be a legitimate product ask.

---

# 9. `readDocument` on an image — REAL, but it **does NOT throw**

**(b)** Global: `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/core/src/globals/read-document.ts` — yield kind `'readDocument'`. Resolver: `resolveUploadDocument` in `/home/vasilis/LMTHING/lmthing/sdk/org/libs/cli/src/server/uploads.ts:290`, wired at `session-manager.ts:423`.

**Behaviour on an image:** it returns a **structured result, not an exception** (`ReadDocumentResult`, read-document.ts:10-22):
```ts
{ ok, attachmentId, mediaType, filename?, kind: 'text' | 'unsupported', text?, truncated?, error? }
```
An image (`classifyKind` → `'image'`, `uploads.ts:38-42`) yields `kind: 'unsupported'` with a human-readable `error`. `extractDocumentText` only handles `application/pdf` via `unpdf` (`uploads.ts:57-69`); a **scanned** PDF has no text layer and is rasterized into per-page **image** uploads (`UploadMeta.pages`) precisely so it can flow down the image→vision path (uploads.ts:23-29).

**(c) Capability: NONE — injected universally** (like `fetch`), per the doc comment in `read-document.ts`. It is *not* gated.

**Delegation to system-vision is NOT automatic** — it is instruction-driven. `system-vision/agents/vision` exists; whether THING routes there depends on `user-thing/thing`'s instruct + its `canDelegateTo`. So an Act may assert (i) the `readDocument` yield resolving to `kind:'unsupported'`, and (ii) a subsequent `{type:'yield', kind:'delegate'}` naming `system-vision` — but the second is a *model-behaviour* assertion, not a runtime guarantee.

**(e) Harness:** `turn().yields` → `{kind:'readDocument'}` + the `yield_resolved` value carrying `kind:'unsupported'`; `didDelegateTo('system-vision')` (`thing.mjs:399-403`).

---

## Bottom line for your Acts

| # | Capability | Real? | Watch out |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `apiCall` | ✅ yield `apiCall`, allowlist-enforced | **Only THING has the grant.** No appbuilder agent can call it. |
| 2 | `include` relations | ✅ in `db.query` / `ctx.db.query` | **Admin `app/data/:table` API does NOT expand.** Assert through an authored API route. |
| 3 | `db.createTable` / `db.addColumn` | ✅ API + `db:schema` granted to automator | **Instructions never teach it** — they route to `writeProjectTable`. This is the bug. |
| 4 | space `form/`+`view/` components | ✅ descriptor + DTS; opt-in `components:` frontmatter | Rendering only in legacy `--web`; **assert `descriptor.type === '<Name>'` via `/asks`.** |
| 5 | `ask()` guards | ✅ | Rejects the promise → `eval_error`; no ask ever opens. |
| 6 | cancelled `ask()` → `null` | ✅ | `onAsk` returning `null` **submits** null; true cancel = `DELETE …/ask/:id` (no harness helper yet). |
| 7 | handler throw / `HttpError` | ✅ worker-isolated | Non-`HttpError` → **500 `internal error`**, message never leaked; pod survives. |
| 8 | history summarization | ⚠️ **runs, but emits NO event** | **Not observable over HTTP.** Default `maxHistoryTurns: 20`. An Act here would be a fantasy. |
| 9 | `readDocument` on image | ✅ but **returns `{kind:'unsupported', error}`, does not throw** | Vision delegation is instruction-driven, not a runtime fallback. |

Now I have what I need. Three findings shape this round:

- **Act III's failure is a real prompt bug**: the automator holds `db:schema` but its `instruct.md` never mentions `db.addColumn` — it explicitly routes column-adds through `writeProjectTable`. That's my system-space improvement.
- **`apiCall` is real** (yield kind `apiCall`, allowlist-gated) but **only THING holds the grant** — good, my Act can assert THING using it.
- **History summarization emits no trace event** — an Act asserting it would be a fantasy. Dropping that candidate.

Let me log progress and generate the new fixture.

# Research links — life-admin household

Real, currently-reachable pages a research step on this household would legitimately fetch
(car-insurance renewal shopping, utility tariffs, the boiler's regulatory service cadence, an
appliance's official warranty terms). Each verified `HTTP 200` by `curl -sS -o /dev/null -w
"%{http_code}"` on 2026-07-13.

1. **https://powerprice.gr/electricity/tariffs/dei-g1g1n-oikiako** — 200. PPC (ΔΕΗ) Γ1/Γ1Ν
   residential electricity tariff, July 2026 pricing (€0.1384/kWh green band ≤200kWh) — what a
   "find me a cheaper electricity plan" research turn would open against the `bills` sheet's
   PPC rows.
2. **https://www.eydap.gr/CustomerSupport/normalrates/** — 200. EYDAP's official water/sewage
   tariff page (Athens water utility) — grounds the `bills` sheet's EYDAP row.
3. **https://www.energycost.gr/en** — 200. RAAEY's official electricity/gas price-comparison
   tool for Greek households — the actual "is there a cheaper option" comparison surface.
4. **https://www.bosch-thermotechnology.com/gr/el/homeowner/products-and-solutions/gas-condensing-boilers/condens-2200-i-w/** — 200.
   Bosch Greece's own product page for its Condens gas condensing combi boiler line — the
   manufacturer support page for the boiler family in `household-ledger.xlsx` / `boiler-service-manual.pdf`.
5. **https://www.dropfix.gr/syntiris-levita-aeriou-odigos/** — 200. Guide to Greece's mandatory
   annual gas-boiler/burner maintenance requirement (Law 3661/2008, ΚΕΝΑΚ) — the regulation behind
   why the household needs the annual visit described in `voice-memo.mp3`.
6. **https://www.bosch-home.co.uk/customer-service/warranty** — 200. Bosch's official appliance
   warranty page (2-year standard EU warranty) — grounds the `expires` column for the Bosch rows
   in `household-ledger.xlsx`'s `warranties` sheet.

| `household-ledger.xlsx` | Authored for this scenario; the PPC/EYDAP tariff figures and the Bosch/Samsung/LG 2-year EU warranty terms are grounded in links 1, 2 and 6 above | New: bills + warranties for the same household (Filolaou 41). |
| `voice-memo.mp3` / `voice-memo.txt` | Generated via Azure OpenAI `tts` (voice `alloy`), round-tripped through the `whisper` deployment for verification on 2026-07-13 | `voice-memo.txt` holds the actual Whisper transcript (not the authored script) — the next-service date and the meter reading both survived the round trip; the engineer's surname anglicized slightly (`Kostas`→`Costas`, `Attikis`→`Akis`) as expected of STT on a Greek proper noun. |

content.content:
await db.update('orders', { where: { id: rows[0].id }, set: { status: '<the new value>' } });
} else {
  // No matching row? INSERT it rather than silently doing nothing.
  await db.insert('orders', { reference: '<the one the user named>', status: '<the new value>' });
}
```

**HARD RULE (updates): actually perform a `db.update`/`db.insert` — and if a target row/column is
missing, ADD it (insert a row, or `writeProjectTable` to add the column) — never report a change you
did not make.** The user opens the app to check; a "done!" with no row changed is the failure. If
`db` is genuinely unavailable (a project with no tables yet), CREATE+seed the table first with
`writeProjectTable(name, schema, rows)` — do not fabricate success.

the screen** — every column the book renders is NULL. The user submitted a recipe through the app's
own form and it came back as an empty card. (This happened, live, in scenario 10.) `writeProjectTable`
now MERGES a redefinition rather than substituting it — so your invented columns can no longer
un-declare the ones holding every existing row — but the merge only keeps the app rendering; it does
not make YOUR row renderable. Only writing the real columns does that.

If the concept genuinely has no column yet, ADD one (`writeProjectTable` with the extra column) —
adding `is_favourite` to `recipes` is right; adding `title` next to `title_gr` is a duplicate that
splits the data in two. And if the table already has CHILD tables for the detail (`recipe_ingredients`,
`recipe_instructions`), fill those too — a JSON blob in a new `ingredients` column is invisible to the
page that renders the child rows.

`writeProjectHook`/`writeProjectApi` ENFORCE this: source whose `db.insert('t', {…})` or
`db.update('t', { set: {…} })` names a column `t` does not have is REJECTED, with the table's real
columns in the error:

> `db.insert('recipes', …) writes a column the table does not have: "ingredients" (did you mean
> "ingredients_text"?). The columns of "recipes" are: id, title_gr, cuisine_id, ingredients_text, …`

content.text: The automator's guidance routes column-adds through `writeProjectTable`. Before I "fix" that, I need to know whether `db.addColumn` is actually *durable* — if it never writes the schema file, teaching the agent to use it would make things worse.
